nouns.animals.dog: A dog is a furry pet that loves to play with you.
It has four legs, a wagging tail, and it barks 'woof!'
You can pet a dog, walk it, and give it food and water.
nouns.animals.cat: A cat is a soft, furry pet that likes to nap.
It has pointy ears, whiskers, and it says 'meow.'
A cat purrs when it is happy and likes to be pet.
nouns.animals.fish: A fish is a pet that lives in the water.
It has shiny scales and fins, and it swims all day.
You watch a fish swim in a bowl or a tank.
nouns.animals.bird: A bird is an animal that can fly in the sky.
It has wings, feathers, and a beak, and it sings 'tweet.'
Some birds are pets in a cage; others fly free outside.
nouns.animals.cow: A cow is a big farm animal that gives us milk.
It has spots, a swishy tail, and it says 'moo.'
Cows live on a farm and eat grass in the field.
nouns.animals.horse: A horse is a big, strong farm animal you can ride.
It has a long mane, a flowing tail, and it says 'neigh.'
Horses gallop fast and live in a barn or field.
nouns.animals.pig: A pig is a round, pink farm animal.
It has a curly tail and a flat snout, and it says 'oink.'
Pigs live on a farm and love to roll in the mud.
nouns.animals.duck: A duck is a bird that swims and waddles.
It has a flat orange beak and webbed feet, and it says 'quack.'
Ducks paddle in ponds and waddle on land.
nouns.animals.rabbit: A rabbit is a soft animal with long ears.
It has a fluffy tail and big feet, and it hops.
Rabbits nibble carrots and hop quickly away.
nouns.animals.lion: A lion is a big wild cat called the king of the jungle.
It has a fuzzy mane around its face and a loud roar.
Lions live in the grasslands far away.
nouns.animals.elephant: An elephant is the biggest animal on land.
It has huge floppy ears and a long trunk for a nose.
Elephants use their trunk to grab food and squirt water.
nouns.animals.monkey: A monkey is a playful animal that loves to climb.
It has long arms and a long tail for swinging.
Monkeys swing in trees and like to eat bananas.
nouns.animals.pets.hamster: A hamster is a tiny, furry pet.
It has round cheeks it stuffs with food, and a little tail.
A hamster lives in a cage and runs on a wheel.
nouns.animals.pets.guinea_pig: A guinea pig is a small, chubby, furry pet.
It has no tail and makes happy squeaky sounds.
Guinea pigs live in a cage and love fresh veggies.
nouns.animals.pets.gerbil: A gerbil is a tiny pet with a long tail.
It has soft fur and likes to dig and burrow.
A gerbil lives in a cage and scurries around.
nouns.animals.pets.parrot: A parrot is a colorful pet bird.
It has bright feathers and a curved beak, and it can copy words.
A parrot lives in a cage and loves to chatter.
nouns.animals.pets.turtle: A turtle is a slow animal with a hard shell.
It can tuck its head and legs inside the shell.
Turtles live near water and move very slowly.
nouns.animals.pets.snake: A snake is a long animal with no legs.
It has smooth scales and slithers along the ground.
A snake moves by wiggling its long body.
nouns.animals.pets.lizard: A lizard is a small animal with a long tail.
It has scaly skin and tiny claws, and it darts fast.
Lizards like to sit in the warm sun.
nouns.animals.pets.ferret: A ferret is a long, playful, furry pet.
It has a skinny body and loves to wiggle and pounce.
A ferret likes to explore and play hide-and-seek.
nouns.animals.farm.goat: A goat is a farm animal that loves to climb.
It has little horns and a wispy beard, and it says 'maa.'
Goats live on a farm and nibble grass and leaves.
nouns.animals.farm.sheep: A sheep is a fluffy farm animal.
It is covered in soft, curly wool and says 'baa.'
Sheep live on a farm, and their wool makes sweaters.
nouns.animals.farm.donkey: A donkey is a farm animal that looks like a small horse.
It has long ears and says 'hee-haw.'
Donkeys are strong and can carry heavy loads.
nouns.animals.farm.rooster: A rooster is a farm bird that wakes everyone up.
It has a red comb on its head and crows 'cock-a-doodle-doo!'
A rooster crows loudly in the morning.
nouns.animals.farm.chicken: A chicken is a farm bird that lays eggs.
It has feathers, a beak, and it clucks 'bawk.'
Chickens peck at the ground for food on the farm.
nouns.animals.farm.turkey: A turkey is a big farm bird.
It has a fan of tail feathers and a red wobbly part, and it gobbles.
nouns.animals.farm.llama: A llama is a tall, fuzzy farm animal.
It has a long neck, big eyes, and soft wool.
Llamas are gentle and live on farms.
nouns.animals.farm.alpaca: An alpaca is a fuzzy animal like a small llama.
It has a fluffy face and super soft wool.
Alpacas are gentle and hum softly.
nouns.animals.jungle.tiger: A tiger is a big wild cat with stripes.
It has orange fur with black stripes and a mighty roar.
Tigers prowl in the jungle far away.
nouns.animals.jungle.giraffe: A giraffe is the tallest animal in the world.
It has a very long neck and brown spots.
Giraffes reach high to nibble leaves off tall trees.
nouns.animals.jungle.zebra: A zebra looks like a horse with stripes.
It has black and white stripes all over its body.
Zebras run together across the grasslands.
nouns.animals.jungle.kangaroo: A kangaroo is an animal that hops on big back legs.
It has a strong tail and a pouch for its baby.
Kangaroos bounce around and live far away in Australia.
nouns.animals.jungle.koala: A koala is a sleepy, fuzzy animal.
It has round ears and a big nose and hugs tree branches.
Koalas snooze in trees and munch on leaves.
nouns.animals.jungle.panda: A panda is a big, cuddly black-and-white bear.
It has round black ears and patches around its eyes.
Pandas sit and munch on bamboo all day.
nouns.animals.jungle.gorilla: A gorilla is a big, strong ape.
It has black fur and long arms and thumps its chest.
Gorillas live in the forest in family groups.
nouns.animals.jungle.hippo: A hippo is a big, round animal that loves water.
It has a huge mouth and short, stubby legs.
Hippos splash and soak in the river.
nouns.animals.sea.whale: A whale is the biggest animal in the ocean.
It is huge and spouts water from the top of its head.
Whales swim in the deep sea and sing songs.
nouns.animals.sea.dolphin: A dolphin is a smart, friendly sea animal.
It is smooth and gray and leaps out of the water.
Dolphins swim fast and click and squeak.
nouns.animals.sea.shark: A shark is a big fish with sharp teeth.
It has a pointy fin on its back that pokes out of the water.
Sharks swim through the deep ocean.
nouns.animals.sea.octopus: An octopus is a sea animal with eight arms.
Its arms are bendy and covered with little suckers.
An octopus squirts away and hides on the sea floor.
nouns.animals.sea.seal: A seal is a smooth sea animal that loves to swim.
It has flippers and whiskers and barks 'arf.'
Seals swim in the sea and flop onto rocks to rest.
nouns.animals.sea.walrus: A walrus is a big sea animal with long tusks.
It has whiskers and two long teeth that hang down.
Walruses rest on cold, icy beaches.
nouns.animals.sea.jellyfish: A jellyfish is a soft, see-through sea animal.
It has a squishy bell and long, dangly tentacles.
Jellyfish drift slowly through the water.
nouns.animals.sea.starfish: A starfish is a sea animal shaped like a star.
It has five arms and creeps slowly along the sea floor.
You can find a starfish in a tide pool at the beach.
nouns.animals.sea.crab: A crab is a sea animal with a hard shell.
It has pinchy claws and walks sideways.
Crabs scuttle along the sand at the beach.
nouns.animals.sea.lobster: A lobster is a sea animal with big claws.
It has a hard shell and a long, curling tail.
Lobsters crawl along the bottom of the ocean.
nouns.animals.forest.deer: A deer is a gentle forest animal.
It has long thin legs, and some have antlers on their heads.
Deer leap quietly through the woods.
nouns.animals.forest.fox: A fox is a clever forest animal.
It has reddish fur and a big bushy tail.
Foxes are quick and sneaky in the woods.
nouns.animals.forest.bear: A bear is a big, strong forest animal.
It has thick fur, round ears, and big paws.
Bears live in the woods and love honey and berries.
nouns.animals.forest.raccoon: A raccoon is a furry animal with a mask on its face.
It has black rings on its tail and clever little hands.
Raccoons come out at night to look for food.
nouns.animals.forest.squirrel: A squirrel is a small animal with a bushy tail.
It has a fluffy tail and scampers up trees.
Squirrels gather nuts and dash around the park.
nouns.animals.forest.owl: An owl is a bird that is awake at night.
It has big round eyes and hoots 'who, who.'
Owls sit quietly in trees and fly silently at night.
nouns.animals.forest.wolf: A wolf is a wild animal that looks like a big dog.
It has thick fur and howls 'awoooo' at the moon.
Wolves live and hunt together in a pack.
nouns.animals.polar.polar_bear: A polar bear is a giant white bear.
It has thick white fur to stay warm in the cold.
Polar bears live on the ice and swim in icy water.
nouns.animals.polar.penguin: A penguin is a bird that waddles and cannot fly.
It is black and white and slides on its tummy.
Penguins live where it is icy and swim to catch fish.
nouns.animals.polar.arctic_fox: An arctic fox is a small fox with fluffy white fur.
Its thick coat keeps it warm and hides it in the snow.
Arctic foxes live in cold, snowy places.
nouns.animals.polar.narwhal: A narwhal is a sea animal with one long tusk.
It looks like a whale with a spiral horn on its head.
Narwhals swim in the cold, icy ocean.
nouns.animals.polar.puffin: A puffin is a little seabird with a colorful beak.
It is black and white with a bright orange beak.
Puffins live on cold rocky cliffs by the sea.
nouns.animals.bugs.bee: A bee is a tiny insect that buzzes.
It has black and yellow stripes and tiny wings.
Bees buzz from flower to flower and make honey.
nouns.animals.bugs.butterfly: A butterfly is a bug with big, pretty wings.
Its wings are colorful and it flutters softly.
Butterflies float from flower to flower in the garden.
nouns.animals.bugs.ant: An ant is a teeny tiny insect.
It has six legs and is very strong for its size.
Ants march in a line and work together.
nouns.animals.bugs.ladybug: A ladybug is a tiny red beetle.
It is round and red with little black dots.
Ladybugs crawl on leaves in the garden.
nouns.animals.bugs.spider: A spider is a little bug with eight legs.
It spins a sticky web to catch its food.
Spiders make webs in corners and gardens.
nouns.animals.bugs.caterpillar: A caterpillar is a little wiggly bug.
It is long and squishy with many tiny legs.
A caterpillar eats leaves, then turns into a butterfly.
nouns.animals.bugs.snail: A snail is a tiny animal that carries its house.
It has a swirly shell on its back and moves very slowly.
Snails leave a shiny trail as they creep along.
nouns.animals.bugs.dragonfly: A dragonfly is a bug with shiny see-through wings.
It has a long skinny body and zips quickly.
Dragonflies zoom over ponds in the summer.
nouns.animals.dinos.t_rex: T-rex was a giant dinosaur from long ago.
It had a huge head, sharp teeth, and tiny arms.
T-rex stomped around millions of years ago.
nouns.animals.dinos.triceratops: Triceratops was a dinosaur with three horns.
It had a big frill on its head and walked on four legs.
Triceratops ate plants long, long ago.
nouns.animals.dinos.brontosaurus: Brontosaurus was a huge dinosaur with a long neck.
It had a long tail and reached up to eat leaves.
Brontosaurus was as tall as a tree.
nouns.animals.dinos.stegosaurus: Stegosaurus was a dinosaur with plates on its back.
It had bony plates along its spine and spikes on its tail.
nouns.animals.dinos.pterodactyl: Pterodactyl was a dinosaur that could fly.
It had big leathery wings and a pointy beak.
Pterodactyls soared through the sky long ago.
nouns.animals.dinos.velociraptor: Velociraptor was a fast, clever dinosaur.
It was small and quick with sharp claws.
Velociraptors ran quickly and hunted long ago.
nouns.animals.reptiles.frog: A frog is a little animal that hops and swims.
It has bumpy green skin and big eyes, and it says 'ribbit.'
Frogs hop by the pond and catch bugs with their tongue.
nouns.animals.reptiles.toad: A toad is a bumpy animal that looks like a frog.
It has dry, lumpy skin and hops slowly.
Toads hop around in gardens and damp places.
nouns.animals.reptiles.alligator: An alligator is a big reptile with a long snout.
It has a strong tail and a mouth full of teeth.
Alligators float in swamps and rivers.
nouns.animals.reptiles.crocodile: A crocodile is a big reptile with sharp teeth.
It has bumpy skin and a long, pointy snout.
Crocodiles swim in rivers in warm places.
nouns.animals.reptiles.gecko: A gecko is a small lizard with sticky toes.
It can climb right up walls and windows.
Geckos like warm places and catch little bugs.
nouns.animals.reptiles.chameleon: A chameleon is a lizard that can change colors.
It has swivel eyes and a long, curly tail.
A chameleon blends in to hide on a branch.
nouns.animals.mammals.more.beaver: A beaver is a furry animal with a flat tail.
It has big front teeth for chewing wood.
Beavers build dams in the river with sticks.
nouns.animals.mammals.more.otter: An otter is a playful, furry water animal.
It is sleek and loves to float on its back.
Otters swim in rivers and slide down muddy banks.
nouns.animals.mammals.more.hedgehog: A hedgehog is a tiny animal covered in little spikes.
It can roll into a prickly ball to stay safe.
Hedgehogs snuffle around looking for bugs.
nouns.animals.mammals.more.cheetah: A cheetah is the fastest animal on land.
It has spotted fur and long legs for running.
Cheetahs sprint super fast across the grass.
nouns.animals.mammals.more.leopard: A leopard is a wild cat with spots.
It has golden fur covered in dark spots.
Leopards climb trees and rest on the branches.
nouns.animals.mammals.more.jaguar: A jaguar is a strong wild cat with spots.
It has a big head and powerful legs.
nouns.animals.mammals.more.sloth: A sloth is a very slow, sleepy animal.
It has long arms and hangs upside down in trees.
Sloths move slowly and nap a lot.
nouns.animals.mammals.more.mouse_a: A mouse is a tiny animal with a long tail.
It has round ears and whiskers and squeaks.
A mouse scurries quickly and nibbles crumbs.
nouns.animals.mammals.more.bat: A bat is a little animal that flies at night.
It has leathery wings and hangs upside down to sleep.
Bats come out at night and sleep in the day.
nouns.animals.mammals.more.chipmunk: A chipmunk is a tiny striped animal.
It has stripes on its back and puffy cheeks for food.
Chipmunks dart around and gather seeds.
nouns.animals.mammals.more.skunk: A skunk is a small animal with a big bushy tail.
It is black with a white stripe down its back.
A skunk makes a stinky smell when it is scared.
nouns.animals.mammals.more.opossum: An opossum is a furry animal with a long tail.
It has gray fur and a pointy little face.
Opossums come out at night and can hang by their tail.
